frameworks.name: evidence accumulation
frameworks.one_line: decisions arise from gradual integration of noisy sensory evidence over time

frameworks.name: drift-diffusion-style accumulator model
frameworks.one_line: trial-by-trial pulse-timing models that separate memory noise from sensory noise

frameworks.name: signal-detection / psychophysical modeling
frameworks.one_line: decision-making framed as discrimination under noisy input

core_question: What are the distinct sources of internal noise that limit evidence accumulation during perceptual decision-making?
key_assumptions: evidence is gradually accumulated over time toward a decision
noise can be decomposed into sensory-input vs accumulator-memory components
richly timed pulse trains identify mechanisms that average rates cannot
rats and humans share a comparable accumulation mechanism
manipulations: stimuli delivered as randomly timed sensory pulses
pulse-by-pulse trial structure for model fitting
key_findings: accumulator's memory was effectively noiseless in both rats and humans
noise associated with incoming sensory evidence was the primary source of variability
pulse-timing analysis distinguishes alternative decision-making mechanisms
interpretations: errors in this task arise mainly from sensory input, not from memory decay during accumulation
pulse-based tasks plus detailed trial models reveal internal properties of decision processes
